title: Hades Project Zeorymer

chunks.content:
Article: Hades Project Zeorymer
[]: Hades Project Zeorymer             Quote Post by Taumiel » Mon Nov 02, 2009 5:39 pm Has someone watched this 80's series? The spanish edition's cover included the motto "the anime that inspired Evangelion", and it is quite true indeed (I'll add some spoiler tags later with similarities)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 02, 2009 5:45 pm I saw some of this at Katsucon back in 2002, sadly I never got around to watching the whole thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 02, 2009 6:04 pm sounds cool

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon Nov 02, 2009 6:27 pm just by reading its wiki, I do sense how it could have influenced EVA also, just looking at this pic could that on the bottom left be a young Ritsuko?

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Nov 02, 2009 7:05 pm Never heard of this show. Also the guy on the top left looks like Maya's brother.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Nov 02, 2009 9:26 pm Lots of shows inspired Evangelion; it's pretty arrogant and inaccurate for any one anime to claim the honor as if they're special. Hell, if anyone, Ideon is the anime that gets that honor.

[]:             Quote Post by Taumiel » Tue Nov 03, 2009 1:23 pm I think the cover's motto is there basically for promotional reasons, but there are a few similarities. Spoiler time! SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Tue Nov 03, 2009 1:44 pm Taumiel wrote: I think the cover's motto is there basically for promotional reasons, but there are a few similarities. Spoiler time! SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Nov 03, 2009 3:43 pm Taumiel wrote: I think the cover's motto is there basically for promotional reasons, but there are a few similarities. Spoiler time! SPOILER: Show

Article: Hades Project Zeorymer
Twilight » Tue Nov 03, 2009 3:43 pm Taumiel wrote: I think the cover's motto is there basically for promotional reasons, but there are a few similarities. Spoiler time! SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Nov 04, 2009 10:19 am That's every mecha anime ever made.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Nov 04, 2009 12:06 pm Not to sound like a an anime savvy smartass, which i'm not, but the whole "estranged father->reclusive son->son suddenly pushed into the cockpit" formula has been a staple of the mecha genre since Mobile Suit Gundam . And if you look through the "references and homages in NGE" pages on the associated Wiki of the forums, you can see that Eva has borrowed from a metric fuckton of places.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Nov 15, 2009 1:25 pm It is worth noting that the whole "God or a Devil" thing is a cliche line used in pretty much everything.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Dec 02, 2009 9:58 am Well, it has wimpy pilot boy. I'm watching it right now and gotta say it has an Eva feeling. There are parts when you see similarities with Eva. I guess I should watch it with time and take notes of what those are.
